body:
Bead: agent-1bdb9b60
Outcome: no_changes — this bead is a tracking record, not an
actionable code change in the agent repository. Its own notes
already declare the disposition; this rationale makes that
disposition explicit and on-record.

What the bead asks for
----------------------

The acceptance offers three alternatives, any one of which closes
the bead:

  (a) routing/catalog change preventing Qwen3.6-27B-MLX-8bit from
      being auto-selected for multi-file refactor work,
  (b) a fix to whatever in the agent's loop is causing
      false-positive tool-call-repeat detection,
  (c) explicit confirmation in the catalog notes that this model
      isn't suitable for agentic refactor work,

plus: "the seven beads cited in the description have a documented
disposition (closed via different harness, deferred, or used as
fixtures)."

Why none of (a)/(b)/(c) is the right action right now
-----------------------------------------------------

(a) Catalog/routing is not the lever for this model. The static
    catalog at internal/modelcatalog/catalog/models.yaml registers
    qwen3.5-27b and qwen3.5-7b but not Qwen3.6-27B-MLX-8bit. The
    omlx provider (internal/provider/omlx/omlx.go) discovers MLX
    models dynamically from the local server's /v1/models/status
    endpoint at request time — they are never registered statically
    in the agent catalog. Adding a "tier=unsuitable" or capability
    constraint to a static entry would not affect dispatch for a
    model that the catalog has no row for. Auto-selection of MLX
    models is governed by the local server's model listing and by
    ddx-side tier policy, not by anything in this repo.

(b) The bead's own notes explicitly defer the tool-loop-detection
    angle: "The one actionable angle that lives here — 'is tool-loop
    detection too eager?' — is a separate small bead that can be
    filed if/when investigation is justified by other models also
    tripping the guard." That justification has not appeared (no
    other harness/model is tripping ErrToolCallLoop). For when a
    follow-up bead is filed, the relevant code is:

      internal/core/loop.go:703-719  — fingerprint + 3-strike abort
      internal/core/errors.go:20-22  — ErrToolCallLoop
      toolCallLoopLimit              — currently 3 consecutive
                                       identical tool-call sets

    Any future investigation should distinguish (i) legitimate
    repetition the guard wrongly aborts, from (ii) genuine model
    failure modes the guard correctly catches. Without that
    distinction, loosening the limit risks masking real capability
    failures with longer runs and higher token spend (the bead
    already cites a 2.65M-input-token 41-minute attempt).

(c) Adding "NOT GOOD AT: multi-file refactor" notes to the catalog
    requires (i) extending the catalog schema (models.yaml has no
    notes/capabilities free-text field today) and its loader, and
    (ii) targeting a row that doesn't exist for the MLX 8bit
    variant. Both are out of proportion to a P4 capability-of-
    third-party-quantized-weights observation that the bead's own
    notes describe as "not a routing/discovery/context-window/
    prompt-size bug in this repo."

Seven cited beads
-----------------

The seven beads referenced in the description
(ddx-0a33bc5f, ddx-915240dd, ddx-9ce6842a, ddx-dd7fce7e,
ddx-0b382233, ddx-d1d7ca17, ddx-ee2cd434) live in
/Users/erik/Projects/ddx/.ddx/beads.jsonl, a different workspace.
This worktree cannot modify their disposition. Their natural
disposition is the standard execute-loop fallback: re-attempt with
a higher-tier harness (claude/opus, gemini-pro, gpt-5.4) on the
next claim, which the queue already does for repeatedly-failed
beads via adaptive min-tier — exactly the policy the bead's
"keep with known low success rate" suggestion endorses.

What a follow-up attempt would need
-----------------------------------

This bead is most useful left open at P4 as a tracking record,
exactly as the notes prescribe. It should only be reopened with
code intent if one of the following occurs:

  - A second, unrelated harness/model combination starts tripping
    ErrToolCallLoop on legitimate work. That justifies the
    "is tool-loop detection too eager?" follow-up bead and the
    accompanying loop.go investigation.
  - The agent catalog gains a capability-constraint schema (e.g.
    via ADR), at which point this bead becomes a concrete first
    candidate for "NOT GOOD AT: multi-file refactor" annotation —
    but only after the omlx provider is changed to consult that
    metadata for dynamically-discovered MLX models, since the
    static catalog rows alone cannot describe them.
  - A reproducer is built that distinguishes false-positive from
    true-positive tool-loop aborts on Qwen3.6-27B-MLX-8bit
    specifically, with a concrete proposal to either raise
    toolCallLoopLimit, change the fingerprint definition, or add a
    backoff window. Today the failure mode (model gets stuck
    repeating one tool call) is consistent with a true-positive
    capability-ceiling abort, not a false-positive guard bug.

No code changes were made in this worktree.
